fresh_facts.claim: US Special Operations forces conducted a night rescue mission in Iran and successfully retrieved an F-15E weapons officer who was shot down, with the service member sustaining injuries but expected to recover.
President Trump issued an ultimatum via social media demanding Iran reopen the Strait of Hormuz within 48 hours or face attacks, with the deadline approximately Monday 10:00 a.m. ET.
Iran shot down a US F-15 and reportedly two additional US planes two days prior, demonstrating persistent air defense capabilities despite five weeks of US-Israeli strikes.
A senior Israeli defense official stated Israel is preparing attacks on Iranian energy facilities within the next week, awaiting US approval.
Approximately half of Iran's missile launchers remain intact with thousands of one-way attack drones available per US intelligence.
US Special Operations rescue mission involved dozens of armed aircraft in a six-week US-Iran war, with Trump directing the mission.
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was killed by US-Israeli strikes on February 28, 2026.
Mojtaba Khamenei was appointed as new Supreme Leader by the Assembly of Experts following his father's death.
Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr, an ex-IRGC deputy, was appointed as Supreme National Security Council chief, replacing killed Ali Larijani.
President Trump stated the US and Iran held a productive conversation about ending hostilities, postponing Pentagon attacks on Iranian energy infrastructure for at least five days.
Iran's Parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf denied direct negotiations with the US, calling Trump's infrastructure strike threats reckless.
Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated Iran is reviewing a 15-point US peace plan delivered via Pakistan, covering Strait of Hormuz reopening, missile limits, sanctions relief, and nuclear rollback.
Pakistan, Egypt, and Turkey served as intermediaries channeling messages between the US and Iran during peace negotiations.
Trump announced Iran agreed to release 20 cargo ships through the Strait of Hormuz starting Monday.
Two men were hanged on Sunday, April 5, 2026, for actions related to January protests, including storming a military site and setting fire to a Basij base.
Severe medicine shortages persist in Iran, with insulin and blood thinners priced at 70 million rials (~$46.70), ongoing for approximately one month.
An Israeli insider predicted that Iranians are weeks away from another uprising against the regime.
IRGC de facto controls the Iranian government amid power struggles following the Supreme Leader's death.
Hardline elements in Iran called for the arrest of former Foreign Minister Zarif, branding him a traitor over his call to end the war.
President Pezeshkian faced hardline backlash over offering conditional terms to end the war.
Iran maintains control over the Strait of Hormuz, which carries approximately 20% of global oil and LNG.
US military operations degraded approximately half of Iran's missile capacity over five weeks, with Iran continuing to retaliate via drones and missiles.
Israel killed an IRGC naval commander during accelerated strikes on Tehran.
The United States planned to add 2,000 or more Marines to the region by Friday following Trump's pause on attacks.
Trump tasked VP JD Vance, Secretary Marco Rubio, Jared Kushner, and Steve Witkoff with phone talks involving a top Iranian official regarding ending hostilities.
Trump stated the US-Iran war should end in days, not weeks.
Peace talks between the US and Iran via Pakistan intermediaries appear unlikely based on low US public war support.
Trump threatened to seize Kharg Island oil and extract enriched uranium from Iran.

regime_fall.current_status: Day 37 of conflict shows simultaneous escalation and diplomatic movement: US conducted deep penetration rescue missions in Iran, deployed additional Marines, and issued 48-hour ultimatums on the Strait of Hormuz, while contradictory signals emerge from Iranian leadership regarding peace negotiations mediated by Pakistan, Egypt, and Turkey.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was killed on February 28, 2026, with his son Mojtaba appointed as successor on March 1, 2026, triggering severe internal power struggles between hardliners controlling the IRGC and moderates seeking diplomatic resolution.
